title: Require negative security tests
description: Security fixes need tests for denied, malformed, cross-scope, replay, and bypass attempts, not only happy-path success.
body: Use when implementing or reviewing security-sensitive changes where a regression could silently grant access or leak data. Do not use to demand exhaustive fuzzing for a tiny non-security edit. Actions: identify the old bypass or plausible attack path, add a focused test that fails without the fix, and keep at least one allowed control case. Validation signals include failing-before/passing-after evidence, clear denied status, and no sensitive body in error paths. Risk boundary: if negative tests cannot run locally, record the gap and escalate rather than marking security validation complete.
